2605.28424.title: Skill0.5: Joint Skill Internalization and Utilization for Out-of-Distribution Generalization in Agentic Reinforcement Learning
2605.28424.paper_summary: Equipping large language models with explicit skills has emerged as a promising paradigm for enabling autonomous agents to solve complex tasks. Agent skills can be inherently divided into general skills for broad cognitive transfer and task-specific skills for dynamic execution. However, existing skill-based reinforcement learning (RL) methods typically force a rigid choice between full externalization, which incurs prohibitive context overhead, and full internalization, which risks overfitting and knowledge conflicts. To address this dilemma, we propose Skill0.5, a novel agentic RL framework that explicitly differentiates skill treatments by combining general skill internalization with task-specific skill utilization. Driven by a dynamic, difficulty-aware router, Skill0.5 streams tasks into distinct mastery tiers to apply tailored optimization strategies: it internalizes general skills via privileged distillation to build a cognitive foundation for hard tasks, while using diagnostic probing on easy tasks to penalize shortcuts and enforce specific skill utilization. Experiments on ALFWorld and WebShop demonstrate that Skill0.5 outperforms both memory-based and skill-based RL baselines, yielding performance improvements across both in-distribution and out-of-distribution scenarios.
2605.28424.paper_summary_zh: 為大型語言模型配備明確的技能已成為使自主代理能夠解決複雜任務的一種有前景的範式。<lb/>代理技能可以本質上分為廣泛認知轉移的通用技能和動態執行的任務特定技能。<lb/>然而，現有的基於技能的強化學習（RL）方法通常迫使在完全外部化（這會產生高昂的上下文開銷）和完全內部化（這會導致過擬合和知識衝突）之間做出僵化的選擇。<lb/>為了解決這一困境，我們提出了Skill0.5，一種新穎的代理RL框架，通過結合通用技能的內部化與任務特定技能的利用，明確區分技能處理。<lb/>Skill0.5由一個動態、難度感知的路由器驅動，將任務流入不同的掌握層級，以應用量身定制的優化策略：它通過特權蒸餾內部化通用技能，以建立解決困難任務的認知基礎，同時對簡單任務進行診斷探測，以懲罰捷徑並強制特定技能的利用。<lb/>在ALFWorld和WebShop上的實驗表明，Skill0.5在性能上超越了基於記憶和基於技能的RL基準，實現在內部分佈和外部分佈場景中的性能提升。
2605.28424.author: Jiapeng Zhu et.al.
2605.28424.authors: Jiapeng Zhu, Jianxiang Yu, Yibo Zhao, Chengcheng Han, Qi Gu, Xunliang Cai, Xiang Li, Weining Qian
